model_narrative: We plan an airline's fleet schedule. We have several aircraft types and a number of routes to serve, and for each route the passenger demand is uncertain and can fall into one of several demand states, each with a known probability. We decide how many aircraft of each type to assign to each route, which determines how much passenger capacity each route gets. Whenever demand on a route exceeds the capacity we allocated, the excess passengers are bumped and we lose revenue. Our goal is to minimize the total expected cost, which combines the operating cost of the assigned aircraft with the expected revenue lost to bumped passengers.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the available aircraft types in the fleet

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the flight routes that must be served

sets.name: h
sets.doc: the discrete demand states on a route, indexing the possible levels passenger demand can take

sets.name: ij
sets.doc: the set of feasible aircraft-type and route pairs; an aircraft type may only be assigned to a route when that pair is a member of this set

sets.name: jh
sets.doc: the set of route and demand-state pairs that can occur

params.name: dd
params.doc: the passenger demand on a route in a given demand state, in passengers

params.name: lambda_
params.doc: the probability that a route experiences a given demand state; for each route these probabilities sum to one across the demand states

params.name: c
params.doc: the operating cost of assigning one aircraft of a type to a route, in thousands of dollars per aircraft; defined only on feasible aircraft-route pairs

params.name: p
params.doc: the passenger seating capacity that one aircraft of a type provides on a route, in passengers per aircraft; defined only on feasible aircraft-route pairs

params.name: aa
params.doc: the number of aircraft of each type that are available in the fleet

params.name: k
params.doc: the revenue lost per bumped passenger on a route, in thousands of dollars per hundred passengers bumped, charged when demand exceeds the capacity allocated to the route

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of aircraft of a type assigned to a route

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total passenger capacity allocated to a route by the assigned aircraft

vars.name: bh
vars.doc: the number of passengers bumped on a route under a given demand state, that is the unmet demand once allocated capacity is exhausted

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total expected cost, combining operating cost and the expected penalty for bumped passengers, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: oc
vars.doc: the total operating cost of all aircraft assignments, in thousands of dollars

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Each aircraft type has a limited number of planes available in the fleet. For every aircraft type, the total number of its planes assigned across all the routes it can serve must not exceed how many of that type are available.

constraints.name: z[j]
constraints.intent: The capacity allocated to a route comes from the planes assigned to it. For each route, set the allocated capacity equal to the total passenger seating provided by all the aircraft assigned to that route, adding up each assigned plane's seating capacity on that route.

constraints.name: dd[j,
constraints.intent: Passengers get bumped only when demand outstrips the capacity a route was given. For every route and every demand state, the demand left over after subtracting the passengers bumped must not exceed the capacity allocated to that route.

constraints.name: oc
constraints.intent: Define the total operating cost. Set it equal to the sum, over every aircraft assignment, of the per-aircraft operating cost on that route times the number of planes of that type assigned to that route.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: Define the total expected cost. Set it equal to the operating cost plus the expected revenue lost to bumping, where for each route you weigh the passengers bumped in each demand state by that state's probability, sum those across the states, multiply by the route's per-passenger revenue loss, and add up over all routes.

query: We're evaluating a gate-slot limit at the route-2 hub: no more than 20 airframes total deployed on route-2 across all aircraft types. Plug that in and tell me where expected cost lands.
expected_pyomo: model.r2_slot_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, 'route-2'] for i in model.i if (i, 'route-2') in model.ij) <= 20)

query: Route-5 absorbs nearly all the bumping in this network — 446-486 passengers per state versus zero on the other routes. Couldn't we require that in any single demand state, no route's bumping exceeds 25% of the total passengers bumped network-wide? Wouldn't that more even distribution beat the current plan?
